sections.heading: Complications
sections.content:
Uncontrolled pain. Management: Palliative radiotherapy (hypofractionated RT: e.g., 8 Gy x 1, 20 Gy x 4-5, or 30 Gy x 10 fractions) and systemic therapy as indicated.24
Impending pathologic fracture (e.g., from bone metastases). Management: Surgery (in patients with good performance status) and/or radiotherapy.24
Impending spinal cord compression. Management: Palliative radiotherapy.24
Psychosocial distress. Management: Empathetic discussion, support, and counseling by the oncology team and specialized services. Refer to NCCN Guidelines for Distress Management.124
Symptomatic brain metastases. Management: Treatment per NCCN Guidelines for Central Nervous System Cancers.10
Hematologic toxicities (neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia) common with platinum-based and combination regimens (e.g., carboplatin/paclitaxel, gemcitabine/cisplatin).140,151
Significant grade 3/4 leukopenia (21%), neutropenia (33%), anemia (25%), thrombocytopenia (20%) with irinotecan/carboplatin.174
Overall higher toxicity with addition of etoposide to paclitaxel/carboplatin.141
Treatment-related adverse events (any grade) in 64.8% of patients (14.6% grade 3–5) with pembrolizumab.188
Common adverse events: fatigue, anemia, diarrhea, nausea. Immune-mediated adverse events (e.g., pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, endocrinopathies) can occur with pembrolizumab and dostarlimab-gxly.188,192,193
Serious treatment-related adverse events in 40% of patients with selpercatinib; most common grade 3+: hypertension (22%), increased ALT (16%), increased AST (13%).195
Grade 3–4 adverse events (e.g., febrile neutropenia) can occur with fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki.201
Chronic radiation-associated dysphagia (7% of patients) in head and neck CUP.235
Radiation-associated toxicities: grade 1 xerostomia, dysphagia, neck stiffness, trismus.236

cards.name: Uncontrolled pain
cards.detail: Uncontrolled pain. Management: Palliative radiotherapy (hypofractionated RT: e.g., 8 Gy x 1, 20 Gy x 4-5, or 30 Gy x 10 fractions) and systemic therapy as indicated.24

cards.detail: Impending pathologic fracture (e.g., from bone metastases). Management: Surgery (in patients with good performance status) and/or radiotherapy.24

cards.detail: most common grade 3+: increased ALT (16%)
